title: NameError
symptom: Python says a name is not defined.
cause: A variable was misspelled or used before assignment.
debugSteps: Compare the failing name with earlier assignments
Run the smallest failing line again

title: KeyError
symptom: Dictionary access fails for a missing key.
cause: Square-bracket access requires the key to exist.
debugSteps: Confirm whether the field is required or optional

title: TypeError
symptom: An operation receives an incompatible type.
cause: Text and numbers cannot be added directly.
debugSteps: Print type(value) for the relevant variables

title: IndexError
symptom: A sequence index is outside the available range.
cause: Python indexes start at zero, so a two-item list has indexes 0 and 1.
debugSteps: Prefer direct iteration when the index is unnecessary

title: AttributeError
symptom: An object does not provide the requested method or attribute.
cause: upper() belongs to strings, not lists.
debugSteps: Move the method call to the correct value
fixed:
words = ['one', 'two']
print([word.upper() for word in words])

title: IndentationError
symptom: Python cannot understand the block structure.
cause: The body of the if statement is not indented.
debugSteps: Find the line named in the traceback
